figures.caption: Figure 2. RES token cost remains constant as dataset size grows exponentially (O(1) proof).
Figure 3. Log-log scaling comparison — RES O(1) vs. Naive O(n). At 16M articles, RES is 1.2 million times cheaper.
Figure 4. Direct token cost comparison across all 20 query types. RES (red) vs. Naive with 50 articles (blue).
Figure 5. Distribution of RES token costs across 95 runs. Tight clustering around μ=1,574 confirms predictable cost.
Figure 1. Detailed RES architecture flow for a sample query. Token costs shown per layer.
